natural_language: Seven blocks — A through G — in three small piles across the workspace. First pile: B is stacked on A, and A is on the table. Second pile: D sits on C, with C on the table. Third pile: F is atop E, with E on the table. Off on its own, G sits by itself on the table. All the top blocks — B, D, F, and G — have nothing covering them, so they're ready to grab. The robot hand begins empty. Your mission: combine everything into one single seven-block mega-tower. The stacking order from the floor upward must be A at the foundation, then B on A, then C on B, then D on C, then E on D, then F on E, and finally G perched right at the summit. One column, seven blocks, exact order.
